Before we plan the sprint, I want to flag one risk. The security team has been silent for eleven days.
They are always slow. Do not be dramatic.
I am not. It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks. That is what it took last year.
And if it does?
The migration cannot start. Worst case, we lose July. It is a dependency, and it is not in my control.
So you are telling me we are going to fail.
No. To mitigate it, we start the tests on the old database this week. The contingency is to go live on the old one in June.
Nobody else raised this. Why you?
Because I saw the warning sign, and I will not downplay it. I need one decision from you by Friday.
I want to flag one risk before we start.
It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks.

I need one decision from you by Friday.

Diogo, before the steering meeting. I know you want to talk about the database approval. Let me tell you how this room works. If you say the word problem, everybody stops listening and starts defending themselves. So do not bring me a problem. Bring me a risk, with three numbers: how likely it is, what it costs us, and what you want to do about it. And bring a decision. If you just say the client is slow, I will say what everybody says: let us wait and see. Give me something to decide, and give me a date. If you do not, in five weeks this is your fault, not theirs.

Before we plan the sprint, I want to flag one risk. The client's security team has not approved the new database, and they have been silent for eleven days. That is the warning sign. It is likely that the approval takes four more weeks, because that is what it took last year. The impact: the migration cannot start, and the go-live moves. Worst case, we lose the whole of July. This is a dependency, and it is not in my control -- but I can mitigate it. My plan is simple. This week, we start the tests on the old database, so we do not lose time. And I need one decision from you today: do we go live with the old database in June, or do we wait for the approval and move the date? Both are acceptable. Doing nothing is not.

Diogo opens the meeting and flags one risk: the security team has been silent for eleven days. Sophia tells him not to be dramatic. He says how likely it is: four more weeks, like last year. He gives the worst case, and the mitigation: start the tests on the old database. Finally, he asks for one decision by Friday.
